projectDescription: A real-time WebGL particle ecosystem simulator where organisms eat, hunt, flee, flock, reproduce, mutate, and evolve — featuring an integrated multi-provider AI Lab Partner that observes, designs experiments, and writes research reports on the living world.
keyFeatures: Real-time WebGL rendering of up to 60,000 organisms at 60fps using Structure of Arrays (SoA) data layout
Spatial hash grid (40px cells) for O(1) neighbor lookups enabling massive organism counts
Four heritable genetic traits (Speed, Aggression, Efficiency, Perception) with configurable mutation producing emergent natural selection
Five behavioral force drives (hunt, flee, flock, food attraction, separation) producing emergent territorial and flocking behaviors
Multi-provider AI Lab Partner supporting OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and any OpenAI-compatible local endpoint
Automated experiment engine that parses AI-proposed JSON protocols, applies parameter changes, captures before/after snapshots, and generates reports
Rock-Paper-Scissors food chain mode creating Lotka-Volterra population oscillations
Configurable food oases that concentrate resources geographically and drive territorial competition
Six scenario presets ranging from peaceful aquarium to extinction event
Procedural Web Audio soundscape reactive to population levels, reproduction, and kills
Session recording via MediaRecorder API with optional audio capture
Settings share via URL encoding and export/import via .primordial file format
Zero dependencies — single self-contained HTML file, no build step, no backend
publicApiSurface: callProviderAPI(messages, systemPrompt) — unified multi-provider AI adapter
getSimSnapshot() — captures full simulation state as structured JSON context
startExperiment(config) — executes an AI-generated experiment protocol
captureSettings() / applySettings(s) — serialize and restore all simulation parameters
shareSettingsLink() — encodes settings as base64 URL parameter
PRESETS object — six named scenario configurations with 20+ parameter values each

Slider IDs: sOrganisms, sFood, sFoodRate, sSpeed, sSpecies, sStartSize, sMaxSize, sOrgSpeed, sLifespan, sReproSize, sOffspring, sOases, sEatRatio, sEatGain, sHunt, sFlee, sFlock, sFoodDrive, sSeparation, sMutRate, sMutStr
Checkbox IDs: chkSameSpecies, chkFoodChain, chkMutation, chkEvoSpeed, chkEvoAggro, chkEvoEfficiency, chkEvoPerception, chkGlow, chkDeathFX, chkShowOases
Experiment JSON protocol: { hypothesis, changes: {sliderId: value}, checkboxes: {id: bool}, duration }
